answer.purpose: To safely extinguish small and controllable fires.
steps: Confirm the fire is small and not spreading rapidly.
Ensure a clear exit route behind you.
Pull the safety pin of the extinguisher.
Aim the nozzle at the base of the fire.
Squeeze the handle slowly.
Sweep side to side until the fire is extinguished.
answer.warning: Do not use water on electrical or flammable liquid fires.
answer.escalation: If the fire is not controlled immediately, evacuate and call emergency services.

answer.purpose: To respond safely when a fire extinguisher fails.
steps: Stop attempting to extinguish the fire.
Maintain a safe distance.
Evacuate the area immediately.
Alert nearby personnel.
Activate the fire alarm.
answer.warning: Do not attempt to repair extinguisher during emergency.
answer.escalation: Contact emergency fire response team immediately.

answer.purpose: To control large fires using continuous water supply.
steps: Ensure at least two trained personnel are present.
Connect the hose securely to the water source.
Check nozzle grip and pressure control.
Open the water supply slowly.
Aim the water stream at the base of the fire.
answer.warning: High pressure can cause loss of control.
answer.escalation: Withdraw if fire intensifies and request fire engine support.

answer.purpose: To supply high-pressure water for firefighting operations.
steps: Remove hydrant cap using appropriate tool.
Connect hose tightly to the outlet.
Ensure hose line is straight and clear.
Open valve slowly to control pressure.
Coordinate with hose team.
answer.warning: Sudden pressure release can damage equipment.
answer.escalation: If hydrant pressure is insufficient, escalate to fire engine support.

answer.purpose: To prevent ignition and injury during gas leak incidents.
steps: Do not operate electrical switches.
Shut off gas supply if safe to do so.
Ventilate the area naturally.
Evacuate all occupants immediately.
answer.warning: Avoid sparks, flames, or mobile phone usage.
answer.escalation: Inform emergency gas response services immediately.

answer.purpose: To safely escape from smoke-filled environments.
steps: Stay close to the floor.
Cover nose and mouth with cloth.
Follow walls or exit signage.
Avoid opening hot doors.
answer.escalation: If exit is blocked, signal for help and remain low.

answer.purpose: To provide breathable air in hazardous environments.
steps: Inspect air cylinder pressure.
Secure mask properly to prevent leakage.
Monitor air supply continuously.
Exit area before air supply is depleted.
answer.warning: Do not enter hazardous area alone.
answer.escalation: Exit immediately if air supply becomes insufficient.

answer.purpose: To protect personnel from heat, impact, and burns.
steps: Inspect PPE for damage.
Wear helmet, gloves, boots, and fire-resistant uniform.
Ensure proper fit and coverage.
Replace damaged PPE immediately.
answer.warning: Never enter fire area without proper PPE.
answer.escalation: Withdraw from operation if PPE is damaged.

answer.purpose: To safely evacuate personnel during emergencies.
steps: Activate fire alarm.
Guide occupants to nearest safe exit.
Assist injured or disabled persons.
Assemble at designated safe point.
Conduct headcount verification.
answer.escalation: Report missing persons to emergency responders.

answer.purpose: To provide immediate first aid for burn injuries caused by fire.
steps: Move the injured person away from the heat source.
Cool the burn under clean running water for at least 10 minutes.
Remove tight items like rings or watches near the burn area.
Cover the burn with a sterile, non-stick dressing.
Keep the person calm and monitor breathing.
answer.warning: Do not apply oils, creams, ice, or burst blisters.
answer.escalation: Seek immediate medical attention for severe or large burns.

answer.purpose: To safely respond to fires caused by electrical equipment.
steps: Switch off main electrical power if safe to do so.
Do not use water on electrical fires.
Use CO2 or dry powder fire extinguisher.
Maintain safe distance while extinguishing.
Evacuate if fire is uncontrollable.
answer.warning: Using water can cause electric shock.
answer.escalation: Call fire services and isolate power supply immediately.

answer.purpose: To control fires caused by cooking oil or grease.
steps: Turn off the gas supply immediately.
Cover the pan with a metal lid to cut oxygen.
Use fire blanket if available.
Keep a safe distance from flames.
Evacuate if fire spreads.
answer.warning: Never pour water on oil fires.
answer.escalation: Call fire emergency services if fire is not controlled quickly.

answer.purpose: To control fires caused by cooking oil or grease.
steps: Turn off the gas supply immediately.
Cover the pan with a metal lid to cut oxygen.
Use fire blanket if available.
Keep a safe distance from flames.
Evacuate if fire spreads.
answer.warning: Never pour water on oil fires.
answer.escalation: Call fire emergency services if fire is not controlled quickly.

answer.purpose: To respond when a person is trapped during a fire emergency.
steps: Do not enter smoke-filled area without protection.
Alert fire rescue team immediately.
Guide trapped person to stay low and signal for help.
Close doors to slow fire spread if possible.
answer.warning: Untrained rescue attempts can be fatal.
answer.escalation: Fire rescue services must handle trapped-person scenarios.

answer.purpose: To safely manage fire incidents in office environments.
steps: Activate fire alarm immediately.
Shut down electrical equipment if safe.
Evacuate employees in an orderly manner.
Assist visitors and disabled persons.
Assemble at emergency meeting point.
answer.escalation: Report to fire safety officer and emergency services.

answer.purpose: To handle fires in industrial or manufacturing facilities.
steps: Trigger emergency shutdown procedures.
Isolate hazardous materials if safe.
Use appropriate industrial fire extinguishers.
Evacuate non-essential personnel.
Maintain communication with fire brigade.
answer.escalation: Immediate fire brigade and disaster response required.

answer.purpose: To extinguish small fires or protect a person using a fire blanket.
steps: Hold the blanket in front to protect hands.
Place gently over the fire or person.
Do not throw the blanket.
Turn off heat source if possible.
answer.warning: Do not uncover immediately after use.
answer.escalation: Seek medical help if person is injured.

answer.purpose: To ensure preparedness through fire drill exercises.
steps: Participate seriously during drills.
Follow evacuation routes.
Assemble at designated area.
Provide feedback after drill.
answer.escalation: Report any safety gaps to management.

answer.purpose: To identify situations requiring immediate fire service assistance.
steps: Call fire services if fire is uncontrollable.
Provide clear location details.
Inform about trapped persons if any.
Follow instructions given by emergency operator.
answer.warning: Delay in calling can worsen the situation.
